title: Origin App Manifest
description: Manifest file for Origin robot apps. Place as origin-app.json at the root of your app directory.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for editor validation.

name.description: Human-readable app name displayed in the dashboard and CLI.

id.description: Unique app identifier (kebab-case). Used in CLI commands and API routes.

description.description: Short description of what the app does.

icon.description: Relative path to an icon image file (e.g. "icon.png").

setup.description: Top-level setup command run during install. Runs before runtime/backend setup. Use for shared dependencies (e.g. "pnpm install").

secrets.description: API keys or credentials the app needs. Users configure these via the CLI or dashboard before launching.

device.description: Device compatibility requirements. Origin checks these before allowing an app to launch on a device.

type.description: Device type this app targets. Use "generic" to support any device.

requiredActions.description: Actions the device must support (e.g. ["set_pos", "reset"]). Launch fails if any are missing.

requiredState.description: State keys the device must report (e.g. ["base_pos_z"]). Launch fails if any are missing.

optionalActions.description: Actions the app can use if available. Missing ones produce warnings, not errors.

optionalState.description: State keys the app can use if available.

runtime.description: Frontend process configuration. Origin spawns this when the app is launched.

entry.description: Entry point directory relative to app root (usually ".").

setupCmd.description: Command to install frontend dependencies (e.g. "pnpm install"). Run during origin install.

buildCmd.description: Build command for production mode (e.g. "pnpm build"). Run during origin install.

devCmd.description: Command to start in dev mode (e.g. "pnpm dev"). Used when launching with --mode dev.

startCmd.description: Command to start in production mode (e.g. "pnpm start"). Used with --mode prod.

env.description: Environment variables passed to the frontend process. Supports template variables: {{origin.url}}, {{device.id}}, {{backend.port}}, {{app.port}}.

healthCheck.description: HTTP path to poll after startup (e.g. "/api/health"). Origin waits for a 200 response before marking the app as running. If omitted, Origin waits for the port to accept connections.

backend.description: Optional backend process (e.g. Python FastAPI server). Spawned before the frontend.

entry.description: Entry point file relative to app root (e.g. "backend/main.py").

setupCmd.description: Command to set up the backend environment (e.g. "python -m venv .venv && .venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt").

installCmd.description: Command to install backend dependencies (e.g. "pip install -r backend/requirements.txt"). Run during origin install.

args.description: Arguments passed to the backend entry point. Supports template variables.

env.description: Environment variables for the backend process. Supports template variables.

healthCheck.description: HTTP path to poll after startup (e.g. "/api/status").

description.description: Human-readable description shown in the dashboard and CLI.

required.description: If true, the app cannot launch without this secret configured.
